title: 16/8 Intermittent Fasting Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Plan your 16/8 intermittent fasting schedule. Calculate eating windows, fasting hours, and break-fast times instantly.
intro: Intermittent fasting works through simple physics: controlling when you eat affects your metabolism and energy balance. Our validated formula gives you a science-backed starting point—adjust based on how your body responds. Calculate your eating and fasting windows instantly. All values updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **16 hours fasting + 8 hours eating window**. Direct calculation from your last meal time. Find when to break your fast and total fasting hours.
useCases: Plan and schedule your daily 16/8 intermittent fasting protocol
Students and health professionals studying fasting methods
Verify your fasting window before starting a new routine
Educational reference for nutrition and fitness planning
Quick daily reference for meal and fasting times
explanation:
Intermittent fasting works by restricting eating to a specific window, allowing your body to use stored energy between meals. The 16/8 protocol means fasting for 16 hours and eating within an 8-hour window. This approach aligns with your body's natural circadian rhythm and helps many people manage their energy intake effectively.

## How the calculation works

Enter your last meal time and select your preferred fasting protocol (16/8, 18/6, or OMAD). The calculator determines exactly when you'll break your fast and calculates your total fasting hours.

The calculator verifies your input falls within reasonable ranges and alerts you to any unusual values.

## Standard macronutrient distribution

| Macronutrient | % of Calories | Energy Density |
|---|---|---|
| Protein | 15-35% | 4 kcal/g |
| Carbohydrates | 45-65% | 4 kcal/g |
| Fat | 20-35% | 9 kcal/g |
| Alcohol | — | 7 kcal/g |
| Fiber | Minimum 25-38g/day | — |

## When to consult a registered dietitian

- If you have medical conditions (diabetes, hypertension, kidney disease) requiring personalized nutrition guidance
- If you're aiming to build significant muscle or compete in sports
- If you struggle with disordered eating patterns (binge eating, restrictive eating)
- If you've been following a fasting plan for 3+ months without progress toward your goals
- To properly plan vegan or vegetarian fasting protocols

## Final notes

This calculation is a reference tool only. For critical decisions (health, medical, legal), consult a qualified healthcare provider. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep information accurate.

faq.a: 16/8 fasting means fasting for 16 hours and eating within an 8-hour window each day. Most people skip breakfast or dinner. It's the most popular intermittent fasting protocol because it's sustainable and effective.
Your body uses energy from your last meal for about 12 hours, then switches to burning stored fat. The 16-hour fasting window maximizes fat mobilization while preserving muscle mass.

faq.q: Is 16/8 intermittent fasting safe for women?
faq.a: Research shows mixed results. Some women thrive on 16/8, while others prefer a 14/10 ratio to maintain hormonal balance. Start conservatively and adjust based on your energy levels.

faq.q: Can I drink water or coffee while fasting?
faq.a: Yes—water is essential and doesn't break a fast. Black coffee and unsweetened tea are also allowed and may enhance fat burning. Avoid adding cream or sugar.

faq.q: When should I exercise with 16/8 fasting?
faq.a: Resistance training is best during your eating window when energy is available. Light cardio during fasting is fine for most people, but always refuel properly afterward.
Both work—it depends on your lifestyle and goals. 16/8 is easier to start with and more sustainable. 18/6 creates a larger deficit for faster results but requires more discipline.

faq.q: How long does it take to adapt to 16/8 fasting?
faq.a: Most people adapt within 1-2 weeks. The first few days may feel uncomfortable and you might experience hunger, but this typically decreases as your body adjusts.

faq.q: What should I eat in my 8-hour eating window?
faq.a: Focus on whole foods: lean proteins, vegetables, whole grains, and healthy fats. Two balanced meals work better than one large meal for most people following 16/8.

faq.q: How many calories do I need daily?
faq.a: This varies by age, sex, weight, and activity level. Generally: adult women need 1,800–2,200 kcal, adult men need 2,200–3,000 kcal. Use a TDEE calculator for your personalized needs.
